cloudNotes: GCC deployment. SIT-based Copilot DLP policies are not supported in GCC — only label-based content blocking is available. Copilot DLP features may have delayed rollout compared to commercial. Validate `Locations` + `EnforcementPlanes` parameters on New-DlpCompliancePolicy and confirm Copilot audit events before demo. Web search is off by default in GCC unless enabled with policy.

presentationFlow.title: Baseline — Copilot without guardrails
presentationFlow.talkTrack: First, let's see what Copilot can do with no DLP policies in place. It can summarize documents, answer questions from files, and use web search. This is the 'before' state.

presentationFlow.title: Guardrail #1 — Block Copilot from labeled files
presentationFlow.talkTrack: Now we activate DLP for Copilot. Content labeled 'Highly Confidential — Restricted' or 'Regulated Data' cannot be summarized, referenced, or reasoned over by Copilot. The label is the boundary — it travels with the document.
showInPortal: DLP policy: Copilot Labeled Content Block
Copilot attempt to summarize labeled file → blocked
Copilot attempt with unlabeled file → allowed
presentationFlow.gccNote: This is the primary Copilot DLP guardrail in GCC. SIT-based prompt blocking is not supported for Copilot in GCC — only label-based content blocking is available. Even without DLP Copilot location, encrypted sensitivity labels independently restrict Copilot access to file content.

presentationFlow.title: Evidence & investigations
presentationFlow.talkTrack: Every Copilot DLP event creates a full audit trail. Security teams can see which file was excluded, which policy was responsible, and who triggered it. This is defensible AI governance in GCC, with feature availability depending on tenant rollout.
presentationFlow.gccNote: CopilotInteraction audit events may not yet be available in GCC. Focus on DlpRuleMatch events which are available.

scenarios.name: Labeled File Protection
scenarios.description: Show Copilot refusing to summarize or reference sensitivity-labeled content
steps: Show a labeled file on SharePoint (Highly Confidential > Restricted)
Ask Copilot to summarize the file
Show that Copilot does not suggest workarounds (copy/paste, relabel)
Show an unlabeled file — Copilot summarizes it normally
Apply a label to the unlabeled file
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Audience sees label-based protection in real time with before/after contrast
automatedArtifacts: DLP policy: Copilot Labeled Content Block

scenarios.name: Audit & Investigation
scenarios.description: Show the full audit trail and investigation workflow for Copilot DLP events
steps: Navigate to Microsoft Purview > Audit > Search
Run pre-configured Copilot DLP audit searches
Show DLP policy hits (DlpRuleMatch events — available in GCC)
Show CopilotInteraction events (if available in GCC)
Show hold query and search results
Tie back to defensible AI use and compliance posture
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Audience sees complete evidence chain from Copilot interaction to compliance record
automatedArtifacts: eDiscovery case with hold and search queries
manualPrep: Generate some DLP alerts before demo for audit data
Confirm CopilotInteraction audit availability in GCC

gccLimitation: SIT-based Copilot DLP policies (blocking prompts containing sensitive info types like SSN, credit card, or PHI) are not supported in GCC. Only label-based content blocking is available for Copilot DLP in GCC. SIT-based Copilot DLP is available in commercial tenants.

userExperienceTable.copilotBehavior: Copilot ignores file — cannot summarize or reference

userExperienceTable.scenario: Attempted web-backed answer using sensitive data
Clean prompt, no sensitive data or labeled files
